findings.evidence: `rg -n "map_toCoalgHom|map_toAlgHom" mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/Bialgebra/TensorProduct.lean code/TauCeti/RingTheory/HopfAlgebra/BaseChange.lean` shows Mathlib's tensor-product bialgebra map has both `map_toCoalgHom` and `map_toAlgHom`, while this PR only adds `map_toAlgHom` at line 183.
findings.fix: Add a `@[simp] lemma map_toCoalgHom` identifying `(map (K := K) f : _ →ₗc[K] _)` with `Coalgebra.TensorProduct.map (BialgHom.id K K : K →ₗc[K] K) (f : A →ₗc[k] B)`.
findings.issue: `map` exports `map_toAlgHom` but not the corresponding coalgebra projection, leaving the public characterization of this `BialgHom` one-sided.

summary: The base-change surface is mostly focused, but the bialgebra-map API is missing its coalgebra-side characterization. Add the parallel projection lemma so users can use `BaseChange.map` at the coalgebra level without unfolding it.
